Title: Successful Google Login
Preconditions: The application is installed on the device
Test Data: Valid Google account credentials
Test Steps:
1. User opens the application
2. User clicks on "Accedi con Google" (Login with Google)
3. User inserts valid Google credentials
Expected Result: User is redirected to the main page of the application and can access all functionalities

Title: Automatic Login for Returning User
Preconditions: User has previously logged in and the device has saved the session
Test Data: None
Test Steps: 1. User opens the application
Expected Result: User is automatically logged in and redirected to the main page of the application

Title: Failed Login with Invalid Credentials
Preconditions: The application is installed on the device
Test Data: Invalid Google account credentials
Test Steps:
1. User opens the application
2. User clicks on "Accedi con Google" (Login with Google)
3. User inserts invalid Google credentials
Expected Result: System displays an error message and prompts the user to re-enter credentials

Title: Successful Credit Card Registration
Preconditions: User has logged in to the application
Test Data: Valid credit/debit/prepaid card details
Test Steps:
1. User clicks on the "Metodi di pagamento" (Payment Methods) icon in the navigation bar
2. User inserts valid card details
Expected Result: Card details are successfully registered and saved for future payments

Title: Failed Credit Card Registration with Invalid Data
Preconditions: User has logged in to the application
Test Data: Invalid credit/debit/prepaid card details
Test Steps:
1. User clicks on the "Metodi di pagamento" (Payment Methods) icon in the navigation bar
2. User inserts invalid card details
Expected Result: System displays an error message and prompts the user to re-enter the card details

Title: Successful Saldo Recharge
Preconditions: User is logged in and has added a payment method
Test Data: Valid card with sufficient balance, recharge amount (e.g., 20 EUR)
Test Steps:
1. User clicks on the payment methods icon in the navigation bar
2. User enters the recharge amount (e.g., 20 EUR)
3. User confirms the recharge
Expected Result: Saldo is updated with the added amount

Title: Insufficient Card Balance for Saldo Recharge
Preconditions: User is logged in and has added a payment method with insufficient balance
Test Data: Valid card with balance lower than recharge amount (e.g., card balance: 10 EUR, recharge amount: 20 EUR)
Test Steps:
1. User clicks on the payment methods icon in the navigation bar
2. User enters the recharge amount (e.g., 20 EUR)
3. User confirms the recharge
Expected Result: System displays an error message stating "Insufficient funds. Please change payment method" and prompts user to change payment method

Title: Locate Nearest Stroller Rack
Preconditions: User is logged in and has enabled location sharing
Test Data: User’s current location with at least one stroller rack nearby
Test Steps:
1. User clicks on the map icon in the navigation bar
2. User moves on the map to locate stroller racks marked with dots
Expected Result: System displays available stroller racks on the map with their locations

Title: View Stroller Details
Preconditions: User has selected a stroller rack
Test Data: Available stroller with details (model, price per hour, features, availability)
Test Steps:
1. User is on the homepage or clicks on the strollers icon in the navigation bar
2. User views the list of strollers from the selected rack
3. User selects a stroller to view its details
Expected Result: System displays stroller model, price per hour, features, and availability status

Title: Successful QR Code Scan to Unlock Stroller
Preconditions: User has selected a stroller and granted camera permission
Test Data: Valid QR code on the stroller
Test Steps:
1. User clicks on the "Scan to Unlock" button
2. Application opens camera
3. User scans the QR code on the stroller for a few seconds
Expected Result: Application recognizes the QR code, unlocks the stroller, and starts the timer

Title: Camera Permission Denied
Preconditions: User has selected a stroller but has not granted camera permission
Test Data: None
Test Steps: 1. User clicks on the "Scan to Unlock" button
Expected Result: System requests camera permission from the user

Title: Invalid QR Code Scan
Preconditions: User has selected a stroller and granted camera permission
Test Data: Invalid or unreadable QR code
Test Steps:
1. User clicks on the "Scan to Unlock" button
2. Application opens camera
3. User scans an invalid/unreadable QR code
Expected Result: System displays an error message "Invalid QR code. Please try again"

Title: Poor QR Code Scan
Preconditions: User has selected a stroller and granted camera permission
Test Data: Valid QR code but not fully scanned
Test Steps:
1. User clicks on the "Scan to Unlock" button
2. Application opens camera
3. User partially scans the QR code
Expected Result: System displays an error message "Please scan the QR code completely"

Title: Successful Payment Upon Stroller Return
Preconditions: User has used the stroller and returns it to a rack
Test Data: Sufficient saldo balance, valid stroller return
Test Steps:
1. User returns the stroller to a rack
2. Timer stops automatically
3. System displays the cost of the ride and deducts it from saldo
Expected Result: System locks the stroller and confirms payment deduction from saldo

Title: Insufficient Saldo for Payment
Preconditions: User has used the stroller and returns it to a rack with insufficient saldo
Test Data: Saldo balance lower than ride cost
Test Steps:
1. User returns the stroller to a rack
2. Timer stops automatically
3. System displays the cost of the ride
Expected Result: System displays an error message "Insufficient saldo. Please recharge" and does not lock the stroller

Title: Illegal Parking Detection
Preconditions: User has used the stroller but does not return it to a rack
Test Data: Stroller not returned to a rack
Test Steps: 1. Timer stops after a predefined time (e.g., 30 minutes of inactivity)
Expected Result: System displays an error message "Illegal parking detected. Additional cost applied. Please return the stroller to the nearest rack"

Title: Submit Problem Report via Email
Preconditions: User has encountered a problem
Test Data: Valid email address, problem description
Test Steps:
1. User clicks on "Segnalaci un problema" in the personal area
2. User fills in the email form with problem description
3. User submits the form
Expected Result: System sends the email to support and confirms submission to the user

Title: View Personal Usage History
Preconditions: User is logged in
Test Data: User has previous stroller usage records
Test Steps: 1. User clicks on the history icon in the personal area
Expected Result: System displays a list of past usages with duration, cost, and stroller model for each entry

Title: View Stroller Status on Map
Preconditions: User is logged in
Test Data: Strollers with different statuses (available, unavailable, broken)
Test Steps:
1. User clicks on the map icon in the navigation bar
2. User clicks on a stroller marker on the map
Expected Result: System displays the stroller’s status (available/unavailable/broken) and allows user to view details if available

Title: Payment via Card for Saldo Recharge
Preconditions: User has logged in and initiated a saldo recharge request
Test Data: Valid card number, expiration date, CVV code, and sufficient funds
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the saldo recharge section.
2. User enters card number, expiration date, and CVV code.
3. User confirms payment.
Expected Result: Payment is processed successfully, saldo is recharged, and a confirmation message is displayed.

Title: Payment Failure with Invalid Card Details
Preconditions: User has logged in and initiated a saldo recharge request
Test Data: Invalid card number, expired date, or incorrect CVV code
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the saldo recharge section.
2. User enters invalid card details.
3. User confirms payment.
Expected Result: Payment fails, an error message is displayed, and saldo remains unchanged.

Title: Search for Nearby Strollers on Map
Preconditions: User has logged in and opened the map view
Test Data: None
Test Steps:
1. User opens the map view.
2. User waits for stroller positions to load.
Expected Result: Strollers within proximity are displayed on the map within 2 seconds (RNF-01, RNF-05).

Title: Select Stroller from Rack and View Details
Preconditions: User has opened the map and clicked on a stroller rack
Test Data: None
Test Steps:
1. User clicks on a stroller rack on the map.
2. User views stroller details (e.g., availability, type).
Expected Result: Stroller details are displayed correctly.

Title: Scan QR Code to Unlock Stroller
Preconditions: User has navigated to the QR scanning section
Test Data: Valid QR code linked to an available stroller
Test Steps:
1. User opens the QR scanning section.
2. User scans the stroller’s QR code.
Expected Result: QR code is scanned within 1 second (RNF-02), stroller unlocks, and user is prompted to confirm rental.

Title: Failed QR Scan with Invalid Code
Preconditions: User has navigated to the QR scanning section
Test Data: Invalid or expired QR code
Test Steps:
1. User opens the QR scanning section.
2. User scans an invalid QR code.
Expected Result: Scan fails, error message is displayed, and stroller remains locked.

Title: Report Damaged Stroller via Help Feature
Preconditions: User has logged in and encountered a damaged stroller
Test Data: Photo of damaged stroller, valid QR code of the stroller
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the Help section.
2. User selects "Report Damaged Stroller."
3. User uploads a photo and enters the stroller’s QR code.
4. User submits the report.
Expected Result: Report is submitted successfully, confirmation message is displayed, and the stroller is marked as unavailable for rental.

Title: View Personal Rental History
Preconditions: User has logged in and rented at least one stroller
Test Data: None
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the "Storico Personale" (Personal History) section.
2. User views past rentals and associated payments.
Expected Result: List of past rentals and payments is displayed correctly.

Title: Account Accessibility at Any Time
Preconditions: User has a valid account with registered credentials
Test Data: Valid username/password, invalid username/password, no internet connection
Test Steps:
1. Launch the app on a device with active internet.
2. Attempt to log in with valid credentials.
3. Attempt to log in with invalid credentials.
4. Attempt to log in with no internet connection.
Expected Result:
1. User is successfully logged in within 2–4 seconds.
2. System rejects invalid credentials and displays an error message.
3. System displays an error message indicating no internet connection.

Title: QR Code Scanning Speed and Usability
Preconditions: Device has a working camera and internet connection
Test Data: Valid QR code, invalid/blurred QR code, no camera permission
Test Steps:
1. Open the QR scanning feature from the main menu.
2. Point the camera at a valid QR code and scan it.
3. Point the camera at an invalid/blurred QR code and attempt to scan.
4. Disable camera permission and attempt to scan.
Expected Result:
1. QR code is scanned within 2 seconds, and the system processes the data.
2. System displays an error message for invalid/blurred QR code.
3. System displays an error message indicating camera permission is required.

Title: QR Scanner Visibility and Accessibility
Preconditions: App is launched on a device
Test Data: None
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the main menu of the app.
2. Locate the QR scanning option.
3. Attempt to find the QR scanning option within 30 seconds of first use.
Expected Result:
1. QR scanning option is clearly visible and easily identifiable.
2. User can locate and access the QR scanning feature without assistance.

Title: First-Time User Guidance
Preconditions: User has never used the app before
Test Data: None
Test Steps:
1. Launch the app for the first time.
2. Follow the onboarding steps or prompts provided.
Expected Result: 1. System provides a guided tour or explanations for key features during the first use.

Title: UI Simplicity and Intuitiveness
Preconditions: App is launched on a device
Test Data: None
Test Steps: 1. Perform a basic task (e.g., logging in, searching for strollers) within 30 minutes of first use.
Expected Result: 1. User completes the task without external assistance, demonstrating intuitive design.

Title: Cross-Platform Compatibility
Preconditions: None
Test Data: Android device, iOS device
Test Steps:
1. Install the app on an Android device and verify core functionalities (e.g., login, QR scan).
2. Install the app on an iOS device and verify the same core functionalities.
Expected Result: 1. App functions identically on both Android and iOS devices without crashes or errors.

Title: Google Maps Integration for Stroller Location
Preconditions: Device has GPS enabled and internet connection
Test Data: Valid location permissions, invalid location permissions
Test Steps:
1. Grant location permissions and search for strollers on the map.
2. Deny location permissions and attempt to search for strollers.
Expected Result:
1. System displays nearby strollers on the map within 2–4 seconds.
2. System displays an error message indicating location permission is required.

Title: Internet Dependency for Core Functions
Preconditions: App is launched on a device
Test Data: Active internet, no internet
Test Steps:
1. Perform a core function (e.g., login, QR scan, map search) with active internet.
2. Disable internet and attempt the same core functions.
Expected Result:
1. All functions execute successfully with active internet.
2. System displays error messages for functions requiring internet when offline.

Title: Data Encryption in Firebase
Preconditions: App is connected to Firebase database
Test Data: None (assume backend is configured)
Test Steps:
1. Log in and perform an action that saves data (e.g., book a stroller).
2. Verify data integrity by checking if the saved data is encrypted using SHA-256.
Expected Result: 1. Data is saved securely in Firebase and encrypted with SHA-256.

Title: Camera Activation Speed
Preconditions: Device has a working camera
Test Data: None
Test Steps: 1. Open the QR scanning feature and measure the time taken to activate the camera.
Expected Result: 1. Camera activates within 2 seconds.

Title: Automatic Google Login After Registration
Preconditions: User has previously registered via Google
Test Data: Registered user account with Google credentials
Test Steps:
1. Launch the application.
2. Observe login behavior.
Expected Result: The application automatically logs in the user without requiring manual credentials.

Title: Manual Google Login for New User
Preconditions: None
Test Data: New user with Google account
Test Steps:
1. Launch the application.
2. Tap the Google login button.
3. Grant necessary permissions.
Expected Result: The user is successfully logged in and redirected to the main application screen.

Title: Add Payment Method
Preconditions: User is logged in
Test Data: Valid payment card details (e.g., credit/debit card)
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the payment section.
2. Enter valid card details.
3. Confirm submission.
Expected Result: The payment method is successfully added and stored securely.

Title: Saldo Recharge Using Saved Payment Method
Preconditions: User has logged in and added a valid payment method
Test Data: Valid payment method, recharge amount (e.g., 10 EUR)
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the recharge saldo section.
2. Select the saved payment method.
3. Enter recharge amount.
4. Confirm transaction.
Expected Result: The saldo is successfully recharged, and the user can now use strollers.

Title: Display Nearby Stroller Racks
Preconditions: User is logged in
Test Data: User location with active GPS/Wi-Fi
Test Steps:
1. Launch the application.
2. Navigate to the map view.
Expected Result: The application displays a map with nearby stroller racks marked.

Title: Select Stroller Rack and View Strollers
Preconditions: User is logged in and map is displayed
Test Data: At least one stroller rack with available strollers
Test Steps: 1. Tap on a stroller rack on the map.
Expected Result: The application shows details of strollers available in the selected rack.

Title: Scan QR Code to Activate Stroller
Preconditions: User has selected a stroller from a rack
Test Data: Valid QR code associated with the stroller
Test Steps:
1. Tap the "Use Stroller" button.
2. Allow camera access.
3. Scan the stroller’s QR code.
Expected Result: The stroller is unlocked, and the user can start using it.

Title: Automatic Saldo Deduction After Stroller Return
Preconditions: User has finished using the stroller and is ready to return it
Test Data: Sufficient saldo balance, stroller usage duration (e.g., 30 minutes)
Test Steps:
1. Return the stroller to the rack.
2. Confirm return in the app.
Expected Result: The cost of stroller usage is deducted from the user’s saldo, and the app closes.

Title: Contact Support Operator
Preconditions: User is logged in
Test Data: Active internet connection
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the support section.
2. Tap "Contact Operator."
3. Enter a description of the issue.
4. Submit the request.
Expected Result: The user receives a response from the first available operator.

Title: View Past Stroller Usage
Preconditions: User is logged in and has used strollers before
Test Data: At least one past stroller usage record
Test Steps: 1. Navigate to the usage history section.
Expected Result: The application displays a list of all past stroller usages with details (date, time, cost, etc.).

Title: Verify Firebase Database Stroller Registration
Preconditions: Firebase database is initialized and accessible
Test Data: Unique stroller ID (e.g., "STRL-001"), availability status ("available"/"in use")
Test Steps:
1. Insert a new stroller record into Firebase with a unique ID and initial availability status ("available").
2. Verify the record is stored with the correct ID and status.
3. Update the availability status to "in use" and verify the change is reflected in real-time.
Expected Result: Stroller record is created, updated, and real-time status changes are visible.

Title: Verify Google Maps API Integration for Stroller Search
Preconditions: Device has internet connectivity, Google Maps API key is valid
Test Data: Valid search query (e.g., "near Central Park")
Test Steps:
1. Enter a search query in the app to locate available strollers.
2. Verify the app displays a map with stroller locations using Google Maps API.
3. Select a stroller from the map and verify its details (ID, availability) are shown.
Expected Result: Map loads with stroller locations, and stroller details are accurate.

Title: Verify Android Minimum Version Compatibility
Preconditions: None
Test Data: Android device with version 6.0 (Marshmallow)
Test Steps:
1. Install the app on an Android device running version 6.0.
2. Attempt to access core functionalities (e.g., stroller search, database sync).
3. Verify the app launches and functions without errors.
Expected Result: App installs and operates correctly on Android 6.0.

Title: Verify iOS Minimum Version Compatibility
Preconditions: None
Test Data: iOS device with version 11.0
Test Steps:
1. Install the app on an iOS device running version 11.0.
2. Attempt to access core functionalities (e.g., stroller search, database sync).
3. Verify the app launches and functions without errors.
Expected Result: App installs and operates correctly on iOS 11.0.

Title: Verify Internet Connectivity Requirement
Preconditions: Device has no internet connection
Test Data: None
Test Steps:
1. Launch the app on a device with no internet connection.
2. Attempt to perform any functionality requiring database access (e.g., search strollers).
3. Verify the app either blocks access or shows an error message.
Expected Result: App restricts functionality or displays an error due to lack of connectivity.
